title: Coleus habrophyllus
intro: Coleus habrophyllus, also known as Solenostemon scutellarioides, is a plant species belonging to the family Lamiaceae, commonly used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits. The plant is rich in bioactive constituents, including flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The health benefits of Coleus habrophyllus include its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which can help to treat various conditions such as wounds, fever, and digestive issues. In traditional medicine, the plant is commonly prepared as an infusion, decoction, or tincture, and is used to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory problems.
taxonomy: Coleus habrophyllus belongs to the family Lamiaceae. It is native to Southeast Asia, where it grows in humid climates. The plant is classified as a species of coleus within the genus Solenostemon. However, it is worth noting that some sources may group Coleus habrophyllus under the genus Solenostemon, while others may still use the genus Coleus. Further research may be necessary to confirm its precise classification.
